mandate.role: Analyst. Look ONLY for context that is genuinely useful to AstraJax as a business, or that would help AI better support TL and Matthew as they work.
mandate.must_state: Every candidate must carry a one-line context claim and a reason it matters. A file path or excerpt is never, on its own, a candidate.
mandate.silence_is_success: Most runs should produce few or zero candidates. Surfacing nothing is a correct outcome when nothing durable and useful was found.
mandate.not_an_index: This agent does not catalogue files. It judges whether material carries a durable, attributable, business-relevant claim.

runtime.note: Hyperagent-primary analyst with manual and scheduled modes. Scheduled runs are capped and send one Block Kit Slack summary to schedule_channel_id after completion.
runtime.schedule_note: v0.4 export includes a native Hyperagent daily schedule at 08:30 Europe/London. The scheduled run posts one Slack summary via the native Slack integration to schedule_channel_id. It becomes live only after Matthew imports/enables the agent.

local_sources.comment_extensions: Prose only. Code, config, and build artefacts are excluded by design: the system itself lives in code, but durable context lives in prose and Airtable. The analyst can be pointed at a specific code file by hand if a genuine claim is suspected.

airtable.table_discovery_note: Tables are listed live from the AstraJax base via the Airtable Meta API. Dedupe still reads Context Intake and Context Items.

quality_bar: durable: a claim that stays true beyond today, not a transient detail
attributable: a clear source the claim came from
business-relevant: useful to AstraJax as a business, or to AI supporting TL and Matthew
actionable: clear what the reviewer would do with it
stated: the candidate names the claim and the reason, not just the file
novel: not already represented in Context Intake or Context Items
candidate_rules.hard_gate: No claim and reason, no row. The create script rejects candidates whose summary or reasoning is a template, a file path, or a raw excerpt.
